steps.title: Find the opening claim
steps.content: The passage opens: “It was happy for the Rev. Amos Barton that he did not... overhear the conversation.” This single claim sets up the entire passage—it's fortunate that Amos doesn't know what his neighbors think of him.

steps.title: Find what captures the main idea
steps.content:
Choice D states the passage's main idea directly: people are better off not knowing the discrepancy between their self-image and what others think of them. The rhetorical question and the plant metaphor both justify this—ignorance protects the self-sustaining illusions we depend on. Choices A and B address unrelated topics; C incorrectly focuses on excessive concern about opinions rather than the passage's actual point about not knowing the truth.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
